text: Tender pork cooked in its own fat until golden and crispy, then chopped and served in tacos with salsa and lime. A bold, flavorful dish that’s iconic across the region.
Triangular tamales made from nixtamalized corn dough, steamed in corn leaves and served with cream, cheese, and spicy salsa. Their rustic texture and earthy flavor make them a Michoacán classic.
A traditional dessert made from curdled milk, sugar, and cinnamon. Soft, sweet, and aromatic — it’s a beloved treat that speaks to Morelia’s colonial past and culinary creativity.
